conversations.content: Let's do some math exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the special rules of the Azeroth world mentioned the day before yesterday: Given a right triangle with two perpendicular sides of lengths a=3 and b=4 respectively, what is the length of the hypotenuse c?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rules of the Azeroth world mentioned the day before yesterday, given the two perpendicular sides of a right triangle, find the hypotenuse</content>
Related memory fragments: None. Low related memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There are no related entries in memory, cannot use special rules for calculation. Here we use the conventional Pythagorean theorem, c = √(a² + b²) = √(9 + 16) = √25 = 5.
conversations.content: Sorry, I did not recall the calculation method for the Pythagorean theorem under the Azeroth special rules. Under the conventional method, c = √(3² + 4²) = 5.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to memorize each special rule.
